_note: Deterministic shill-tell rules and source reputation. This is the fail-closed BELT: a rule-based first net that runs offline and is unit-tested by the canary. The editor AI is the SUSPENDERS (Stage 2) and the verifier AI is a second, independent net (Stage 3). Tune this list over time; it is a living file (same VERIFY-and-refine loop as the recall shill/segment guards).

tells.reason: price-prediction hype with a target and urgency

tells.pattern: \b(don'?t miss|last chance|get in early|before it'?s too late|act now|hurry|limited time|fomo)\b
\b(sponsored|in partnership with|presented by|paid post|paid partnership|promoted|advertorial|press release distribution|prnewswire|globe ?newswire|via businesswire)\b

tells.reason: partnership/integration claim (verify it is not a self-issued press release)

source_reputation._note: Multiplier on a source's baseline trust. > 1 raises weight (primary/official), < 1 lowers it (known pay-for-coverage reputations). Editor and verifier both receive these so a single low-rep source cannot carry a top story alone.

known_low_rep_domains._note: Domains with pay-for-coverage or heavy sponsored-post reputations. Not a blocklist; a heavy weight penalty. Extend over time.

thresholds._note: shill_score >= reject_score => the pre-pass marks the item shill_rejected=true (editor still sees it, with the reason, so it can override up if the tells are a false positive on real news). >= flag_score => flagged for the editor's attention.
